[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: cancelRoot] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER who is an authorized operator of maker call cancelRoot(maker, root) to permanently invalidate all pending EcrecoverRatifier-gated offers under that root, under the precondition that maker has called setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, maker) and maker's only exit path (e.g. a reduce-only sell offer) relies on that root, triggering the call sequence [setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, maker), cancelRoot(maker, root), take(offer, ratifierData, ...) -> RootCanceled revert], violating the invariant that a maker's authorized operator must not be able to permanently freeze maker's own pending offers, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of maker's credit or collateral locked in offers that can never be filled? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: cancelRoot / isRatified] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER who is an authorized operator of maker call cancelRoot(maker, root) on behalf of maker without maker's consent, under the precondition that maker authorized attacker for an unrelated purpose (e.g. collateral management) and maker has active EcrecoverRatifier-gated lend offers, triggering the call sequence [maker: setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, maker), attacker: cancelRoot(maker, root), borrower: take(lenderOffer, ratifierData, ...) -> RootCanceled revert], violating the invariant that cancelRoot must only be callable by the maker or an explicitly root-cancellation-consenting delegate, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of all lender offers under that root, blocking borrowers from repaying via take and lenders from withdrawing credit? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER cause a permanent offer freeze by submitting ratifierData where leafIndex has bits set at positions >= proof.length, triggering HashLib.isLeaf to revert with LeafIndexOutOfRange, under the precondition that a valid offer exists in a Merkle tree and the taker submits leafIndex = 2**proof.length (one bit too high), triggering the call sequence [take(offer, abi.encode(sig, root, 2**proof.length, proof), units, ...) -> isRatified -> isLeaf -> LeafIndexOutOfRange revert], violating the invariant that a valid proof must always be verifiable, causing scoped impact: DoS of take for any offer if taker submits malformed leafIndex, blocking settlement and repay flows? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER permanently freeze a maker's offers by revoking the maker's authorization of EcrecoverRatifier in Midnight (setIsAuthorized(ecrecoverRatifier, false, maker)), under the precondition that maker previously authorized EcrecoverRatifier and has active signed offers, triggering the call sequence [maker: setIsAuthorized(ecrecoverRatifier, false, maker), taker: take(offer, ratifierData, ...) -> Midnight checks isAuthorized[maker][ratifier] -> RatifierUnauthorized revert], violating the invariant that a maker's signed offers must remain fillable until expiry or explicit cancellation, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of all maker's EcrecoverRatifier-gated offers, blocking any dependent lender withdrawal or borrower repayment flows? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Permanent or long-term freezing of user funds caused by broken withdraw, repay, liquidate, claim fee, or settlement paths'] [Function: isRatified] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER who is an authorized operator of maker revoke maker's EcrecoverRatifier authorization via Midnight.setIsAuthorized(ecrecoverRatifier, false, maker) on behalf of maker, under the precondition that attacker is authorized by maker for any purpose and maker has active EcrecoverRatifier-gated offers, triggering the call sequence [attacker: midnight.setIsAuthorized(ecrecoverRatifier, false, maker), taker: take(offer, ratifierData, ...) -> RatifierUnauthorized revert], violating the invariant that only the maker should be able to invalidate their own ratifier authorization, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of all maker's pending offers and any dependent fund flows?
